query:
Mostly yes, but two gaps worth flagging before coding starts:
                                                                 
  1. dead_knowledge.rs is missing from the plan                                                                        
                                                                                                                       
  The issue (#437) covers both recurring_friction and dead_knowledge extractors. The fix plan only addresses           
  recurring_friction. dead_knowledge.rs produces "Possible dead knowledge: {title}" entries through the same           
  ExtractionRule::evaluate() → ProposedEntry path. It needs the same treatment — remove the ExtractionRule impl,       
  replace with compute_dead_knowledge_recommendations() -> Vec<String>, remove from default_extraction_rules(). The
  plan should cover both or explicitly defer dead_knowledge with a reason.

  2. How do friction_signals reach context_status?                                                                     
   
  The plan adds friction_signals: Vec<String> to TickMetadata (background tick) and then appends them in mcp/tools.rs. 
  But context_status is called on-demand, not as part of the tick. TickMetadata lives and dies within a single tick
  execution.                                                                                                           
                                                                
  How does the signal cross from the tick to the status call? The plan needs to make this explicit — either:           
  - A Arc<RwLock<Vec<String>>> shared between the tick and status service (simplest, same pattern as
  PhaseFreqTableHandle)                                                                                                
  - Stored in COUNTERS and read at status time                  
  - Recomputed from observation data at status call time (no shared state needed, but costs a DB read)                 
                                                                                                                       
  The rest is sound: removing the ExtractionRule impl, 4→3 rules, COUNTERS-gated one-shot quarantine, and the          
  regression test are all correct calls.                     - Rereview these additional concerns and re-evaluate plan

entries.title: ADR-002 col-025: synthesize_from_session Returns current_goal Directly
ADR-001 crt-029: New Module nli_detection_tick.rs for Background Inference Tick
contradiction scan O(N) ONNX cost: cache in ContradictionScanCache, tick writes every 4th tick, context_status reads cache
ExtractionRule must not perform side effects: ephemeral signals belong in compute functions inside spawn_blocking, not in ProposedEntry output
ADR-006: Convert ExtractionRule::evaluate() to async fn
